acceptance_targets: [ch15-f01] PASS if the revised casualty section anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:159-166 includes concrete bodily action, sensory detail, and a decision-cost for Ina, and the bulletin section anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:515-518 includes at least one named callback to that embodied event. FAIL if harm remains primarily count updates and secondhand report verbs.

[ch15-f02] PASS if each revised block (around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:417-441 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:517-585) contains at least one explicit internal sensation/thought from Ina and one live interaction consequence triggered by her choice. FAIL if either block still reads primarily as administrative summary.

[ch15-f07] PASS if revised post-381 material includes a direct confrontation with an opposing actor and Ina makes a visible either/or decision with immediate cost before the St. Mary's call. FAIL if opposition remains mediated only through broadcasts, rumors, or paperwork.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised casualty section anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:159-166 includes concrete bodily action, sensory detail, and a decision-cost for Ina, and the bulletin section anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:515-518 includes at least one named callback to that embodied event. FAIL if harm remains primarily count updates and secondhand report verbs.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift: the chapter's core harm (panic deaths and detentions) is repeatedly conveyed as relayed reports and aggregate counts, so consequence is often abstracted at the exact moments that need embodied scene presence.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:159, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:163, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:165, replace at least one report-only casualty beat with on-page action Ina directly witnesses or physically manages. Then revise the recap beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:419 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:515 so they tie numbers to named human aftermath already shown, not just tallies.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if each revised block (around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:417-441 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:517-585) contains at least one explicit internal sensation/thought from Ina and one live interaction consequence triggered by her choice. FAIL if either block still reads primarily as administrative summary.
findings.problem: Timidity: after emotional peaks, prose repeatedly retreats into board-management and accounting language, softening immediate emotional and physical reality instead of sustaining pressure in-scene.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the ledger-heavy stretches at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:417-441 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:517-585 to keep operational tracking but anchor each block in immediate body-level stakes (fatigue, panic control, fear of exposure) and interpersonal fallout from Ina's choices.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised post-381 material includes a direct confrontation with an opposing actor and Ina makes a visible either/or decision with immediate cost before the St. Mary's call. FAIL if opposition remains mediated only through broadcasts, rumors, or paperwork.
findings.problem: After Kwon's on-air move, antagonistic force is mostly off-page rumor and bulletin pressure; the back half lacks a direct opposing encounter that forces a fresh irreversible choice before the handoff to chapter 16.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add one on-page counterforce beat in the post-Kwon section (target snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:403-440 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:491-531) where an identifiable actor tries to block, seize, or expose Ina's records, forcing a binary decision that changes what she carries into snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:587.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
